questions.question: A researcher wants to compare the average height of males and females in a population. A sample of 100 males and 100 females has a mean height of 175cm and 160cm respectively. The population standard deviation is known to be 5cm. Calculate the p-value for a two-tailed test comparing the means of the two groups. (Assume that the null hypothesis is the means are equal.)
questions.hint: Use t-distribution and calculate the t-statistic before finding the p-value.

questions.question: A machine learning model is trained on 10 features to predict a binary outcome. After feature selection, 3 features are selected and the model has an accuracy of 90% on the training set. Calculate the degrees of freedom for the t-test comparing the accuracy of the model with the accuracy of a baseline model that uses all features. (Assume that the null hypothesis is that the accuracy of the selected features is equal to the accuracy of all features.)
questions.hint: Use the fact that the degrees of freedom for a t-test is n-1, where n is the number of samples.

questions.question: A researcher conducts 5 identical experiments to compare the effect of two different treatments on a continuous outcome variable. Each experiment has 10 samples. The null hypothesis is that the treatments have no effect on the outcome variable. Calculate the p-value for the test of the overall effect of the treatments. (Assume that the tests are independent and that the null hypothesis is true for each experiment.)
questions.hint: Use the Bonferroni method to calculate the p-value.
